items.prompt: Evaluate lim x->1 [x^3 - x^2 + 1] by substitution.
items.srcLabel: NCERT Class 11 Maths Ch.12 Example 1(i) - limit of a polynomial

items.prompt: Evaluate lim x->2 (x^3 - 4x^2 + 4x)/(x^2 - 4). (It is the 0/0 form; cancel the common factor first.)
items.srcLabel: NCERT Class 11 Maths Ch.12 Example 2(ii) - rational-function limit, 0/0 cancellation

items.prompt: Using the standard limit (x^n - a^n)/(x - a) -> n a^(n-1), evaluate lim x->1 (x^15 - 1)/(x^10 - 1).
items.srcLabel: NCERT Class 11 Maths Ch.12 Example 3(i) - standard limit (x^n - a^n)/(x - a)

items.prompt: Using lim x->0 (sin x)/x = 1, evaluate lim x->0 (sin 4x)/(sin 2x).
items.srcLabel: NCERT Class 11 Maths Ch.12 Example 4(i) - trigonometric limit

items.prompt: Using the first-principle definition f'(a) = lim h->0 [f(a+h) - f(a)]/h, find the derivative of f(x) = 3x at x = 2.
items.srcLabel: NCERT Class 11 Maths Ch.12 Example 5 - derivative from first principle

items.prompt: By the power rule, the derivative of f(x) = x^n is:
items.srcLabel: NCERT Class 11 Maths Ch.12 Theorem 6 - power rule

items.prompt: Using the quotient rule, the derivative of f(x) = (x + 1)/x is -1/x^2. Find its value at x = 1.
items.srcLabel: NCERT Class 11 Maths Ch.12 Example 15 - quotient rule

items.prompt: For f(x) = 1 + x + x^2 + x^3 + ... + x^50, differentiate term by term and evaluate f'(1).
items.srcLabel: NCERT Class 11 Maths Ch.12 Example 14 - derivative of a polynomial at a point

items.prompt: The derivative of sin x with respect to x is:
items.srcLabel: NCERT Class 11 Maths Ch.12 Example 16 / Summary - d/dx(sin x) = cos x

items.prompt: For f(x) = 1 if x <= 0 and f(x) = 2 if x > 0, state the left-hand and right-hand limits at x = 0 and explain whether lim x->0 f(x) exists.

expected.rubric: The left-hand limit lim x->0^- f(x) = 1 (dictated by x <= 0) and the right-hand limit lim x->0^+ f(x) = 2 (dictated by x > 0). Since the two one-sided limits do not coincide, lim x->0 f(x) does not exist, even though f(0) is defined.
markPoints: Limit does not exist because the one-sided limits differ

items.srcLabel: NCERT Class 11 Maths Ch.12 Sec 12.3 - one-sided limits and existence (step function)
